explanation: This command uses PowerShell to decompress Gzip data using the GZipStream class, which matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It is a direct simulation of the behavior described in the rule.

This command uses an alternate syntax for the PowerShell command-line interface (-c instead of -Command) and encodes the input data as Base64, which is a safe mutation technique. It still matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command adds the -WindowStyle Hidden parameter, which is a common obfuscation technique. It does not change the core behavior of the command and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command uses an alternative method to represent the input data (ASCII encoding) instead of directly passing it as a string. It is a safe mutation technique and still matches the Sigma rule.
